prompt: Completa con il diminutivo: 'Aspetta un ____, arrivo subito.' (momento)
explanation: 'Aspetta un momentino, arrivo subito' means 'Wait just a moment, I'm coming.' A suffix can shrink a noun and warm it up, giving an alterato. The diminutivo -ino turns momento into momentino, a small, friendly moment. Usage fixes the suffix here: it is momentino, not momentetto or momentuccio.

prompt: Completa con il diminutivo: 'Il ____ dorme nella cesta.' (gatto piccolo)
explanation: 'Il gattino dorme nella cesta' means 'The kitten is sleeping in the basket.' The diminutivo -ino turns gatto into gattino, a small cat. This alterato is fully transparent: the meaning is still 'a little gatto', which is exactly what separates it from a falso alterato.

prompt: Tua sorella è adulta, ma la chiami 'sorellina'. Il diminutivo qui esprime soprattutto: statura piccola o affetto? Rispondi 'affetto' o 'statura'.
explanation: 'La chiamo sorellina' means 'I call her my little sister.' The diminutivo often carries affection rather than size: sorellina is warmth, not a claim that she is short. The same affective colour is in un attimino ('just a tick'). Reading -ino as 'small' only would miss the point.

prompt: Completa con il diminutivo: 'Da bambino ero un ____ molto vivace.' (ragazzo)
explanation: 'Da bambino ero un ragazzino molto vivace' means 'As a child I was a very lively kid.' The diminutivo -ino turns ragazzo into ragazzino, a young boy. Watch the wrong direction: ragazzone is the accrescitivo, a big lad, the opposite of what the sentence wants.

prompt: Il diminutivo di 'cucchiaio' è 'cucchiaino'. Oggi 'cucchiaino' indica un piccolo cucchiaio qualsiasi, o un oggetto preciso (il cucchiaino da caffè)? Rispondi 'oggetto preciso' o 'piccolo cucchiaio'.
explanation: A cucchiaino is a teaspoon. Some alterati harden into their own dictionary words: the diminutivo has stopped meaning simply 'a small X' and now names one precise object. This is the lexicalisation limit, and it is why you produce the common attested forms and only recognise the rest rather than coining freely.

prompt: Completa con il diminutivo affettuoso: 'Il mio ____ ha due anni meno di me.' (fratello)
explanation: 'Il mio fratellino ha due anni meno di me' means 'My little brother is two years younger than me.' The diminutivo -ino gives fratello the form fratellino. As with sorellina, the alterato reads as affection as much as size: a fratellino is your dear younger brother, not merely a short one.

prompt: Completa con l'accrescitivo: 'Non è un libro, è un ____!' (libro molto grande)
explanation: 'Non è un libro, è un librone!' means 'It's not a book, it's a great big tome!' The accrescitivo -one makes a noun big, turning libro into librone. Keep the direction straight: libretto is the diminutivo (a booklet) and libraccio is the peggiorativo (a trashy book); neither means 'large'.

prompt: Completa con l'accrescitivo e l'articolo giusto: 'Hanno chiuso ____ del palazzo.' (la porta grande)
explanation: 'Hanno chiuso il portone del palazzo' means 'They shut the building's main door.' The accrescitivo -one enlarges la porta, and it also flips the gender: feminine la porta becomes masculine il portone. Many feminine bases do this (la palla, il pallone; la stanza, lo stanzone), so the article is part of the answer, not just the suffix.
examiner_note: Hot spot: gender shift under -one. common_miss 'la portona' keeps the feminine and is caught. Candidate for a proposed gender_shift sub-bucket (see bucket_suggestions). Crit-18 'guard contains an any_phrase': the bare augmentative was removed so the wrong-gender attempt falls through to must_not_include instead of matching the positive. The article is the diagnostic, so the full form is required.

prompt: Completa con l'accrescitivo e l'articolo giusto: 'Si è comprato ____ per la famiglia.' (la macchina grande)
explanation: 'Si è comprato il macchinone per la famiglia' means 'He bought himself a huge car for the family.' Feminine la macchina takes the accrescitivo -one and turns masculine: il macchinone. The gender shift is the trap; la macchinona is the miss to avoid.
examiner_note: Hot spot: gender shift under -one; parallels wf_aug_02. Crit-18 'guard contains an any_phrase': the bare augmentative was removed so the wrong-gender attempt falls through to must_not_include instead of matching the positive. The article is the diagnostic, so the full form is required.

prompt: Completa con la forma peggiorativa: 'Che ____! Piove e tira vento.' (tempo)
explanation: 'Che tempaccio!' means 'What awful weather!' The peggiorativo -accio judges quality, giving tempo the form tempaccio, foul weather. It is not about size: tempone is not the point, and the diminutivo tempino would be the wrong colour entirely.

prompt: Completa con la forma peggiorativa: 'Non dire ____ davanti ai bambini!' (parola brutta)
explanation: 'Non dire parolacce davanti ai bambini!' means 'Don't say swear words in front of the children!' The peggiorativo -accia turns parola into parolaccia, a swear word: the suffix marks bad quality, not size. Contrast parolona (a big, long word) and parolina (a nice little word).

prompt: 'Ragazzaccio' descrive un ragazzo: molto grande, o che si comporta male? Rispondi 'si comporta male' o 'molto grande'.
explanation: A ragazzaccio is a badly-behaved boy, a rascal. The peggiorativo -accio judges quality, not size: it says the boy behaves badly, not that he is big. The accrescitivo ragazzone would be the big lad. Same base ragazzo, different suffix, different meaning.

prompt: 'Il pittore ha messo la tela sul cavalletto.' La parola 'cavalletto' è:
explanation: 'Il pittore ha messo la tela sul cavalletto' means 'The painter put the canvas on the easel.' Cavalletto is an easel, an independent word: only its shape looks like cavallo + -etto. This is a falso alterato, where the meaning no longer contains the base word. The test is exactly that: does 'small horse' survive in the meaning? It does not.

prompt: Completa con il prefisso intensivo 'stra-': 'Non saliamo, il treno è ____.' (pieno)
explanation: 'Il treno è strapieno' means 'The train is packed.' The prefisso intensivo stra- pushes an adjective to the top: pieno becomes strapieno, absolutely full. It works like an absolute superlative (pienissimo) but is built with a prefix instead of -issimo; the superlative frame itself is the comparison topic's.

prompt: Completa con 'super-': 'Hanno comprato un computer ____.' (veloce)
explanation: 'Un computer superveloce' means 'A super-fast computer.' The prefisso intensivo super- raises veloce to superveloce, very fast indeed. Since super- already intensifies, you do not stack -issimo on top: superveloce, not supervelocissimo.

prompt: Completa con 'arci-': 'Sono ____ di sentire sempre le stesse scuse.' (stufo)
explanation: 'Sono arcistufo di sentire sempre le stesse scuse' means 'I'm utterly fed up of always hearing the same excuses.' The prefisso intensivo arci- takes stufo to arcistufo, thoroughly fed up. Note that arcistufo is already an absolute superlative in force (like stufissimo), the frame comparison owns; here you just produce the prefixed form.

prompt: Completa con 'ultra-': 'Vivono in un edificio ____.' (moderno)
explanation: 'Vivono in un edificio ultramoderno' means 'They live in an ultra-modern building.' The prefisso intensivo ultra- lifts moderno to ultramoderno, extremely modern. Like stra-, arci- and super-, it attaches to the adjective and needs no separate 'molto'.

prompt: 'strapieno' e 'stracarico' esistono. Si può formare liberamente 'strasedia' (stra + sedia, un nome)?

markpoints.label: Intensifiers attach to adjectives, not free nouns

explanation: No: strasedia is not Italian. The intensifying prefixes attach to adjectives (strapieno, stracarico, straricco), not freely to nouns. The productive pattern is prefisso + adjective; extending it to a noun overgeneralises. A few fixed verbs like stravedere exist, but you learn those as set words, not by rule.

prompt: Un 'neolaureato' è una persona che si è appena laureata.
explanation: A neolaureato is someone who has just graduated, so the statement is true. The prefisso neo- means 'newly': neo- + laureato = newly graduated. The same neo- gives neonato (newborn) and neologismo (a newly-coined word).

prompt: Un 'neonato' è un bambino appena nato.
explanation: A neonato is a newborn baby, so the statement is true. Neo- means 'newly' and nato means 'born': neo- + nato = newly born. Recognising neo- as 'new' is the skill; the pattern is fully transparent here.

prompt: 'Neoclassico' significa 'contro il classico'.
explanation: False. Neo- means 'new', not 'against': neoclassico is the new or revived classical style, not the opposite of classical. Reading neo- as a change of meaning rather than as 'newness' is the common slip this item catches.

prompt: Completa con 'ex' (= precedente): 'Ho incontrato il mio ____ al supermercato.' (marito precedente)
explanation: 'Ho incontrato il mio ex marito al supermercato' means 'I ran into my ex-husband at the supermarket.' Ex means 'former' and stands as a separate word (or hyphenated): il mio ex marito, il mio ex-marito. Unlike the bound prefixes, ex is written apart from its noun.

prompt: Il 'neo-presidente' e l' 'ex presidente' sono la stessa persona?

markpoints.label: neo- (new) vs ex (former) point opposite ways

explanation: No: they are opposites. The neo-presidente is the new president just taking office; the ex presidente is the former one who has left. Neo- means 'new' and ex means 'former', so the two prefixes point in opposite directions even on the same noun.

prompt: Completa con il diminutivo in -otto: 'Il ____ è caduto dal nido.' (passero, il piccolo)
explanation: 'Il passerotto è caduto dal nido' means 'The young sparrow fell out of the nest.' The diminutivo -otto is the one that most often marks a young animal rather than merely a small thing: passero gives passerotto, and the same suffix gives aquilotto (eaglet) and leprotto (young hare). Usage fixes it: passerotto, not passerino.
examiner_note: Second pass, approved at v5. One -otto item: clean non-lexicalised bases are thin, so this covers the suffix named in the dispatch scope without forcing more.
